name: chr9_pericentric_inversion_polymorphism
description: Pericentric inversion polymorphism inv(9)(p11q13), one of the most common cytogenetic variants in humans (~1-3% of the population). Encompasses the chr9 centromere and the 9q12 HSat3 heterochromatin block. Mostly benign but complicates SV mapping near the chromosome 9 centromere.

architecture.internal_structure: Pericentric inversion spanning the centromere and 9q12 heterochromatin (HSat3); breakpoints in alpha-satellite and pericentric satellite; population frequency 1-3%.
